451.name: Mom150+Sto14+Top3 (cc3_452.py)
451.description: A hybrid trend-score rotator that blends a leveraged Nasdaq ETF, a large-cap universe sleeve, and a cash fallback based on three binary signals. Each day it tallies how many of a 200-bar median regime filter, a 150-day price momentum, and a Stochastic-K midline cross are bullish, then allocates proportionally. At maximum conviction it goes all-in on the 3x leveraged ETF; at zero conviction it splits between the large-cap sleeve and cash.
451.overfit_notes: Median-of-200 regime (non-SMA), 150-day lookback momentum, Stochastic(14,3,3) with K>50 midline. Two out of three signals use non-canonical periods. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: Score 3/3 → 100% TQQQ; score 2/3 → 50% TQQQ + 50% large-cap Top 3; score 1/3 → 100% large-cap Top 3; score 0/3 → 50% large-cap Top 3 + 50% BIL

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Stochastic(14,3,3) %K > 50 (bullish vote); 150-day price momentum positive (bullish vote)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / large-cap Top 3 by market cap / BIL

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 100 by dollar volume → Top 3 by market cap

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open

452.name: Mom20+Sto50+Top3 (cc3_453.py)
452.description: A hybrid trend-score rotator using three binary conditions: a 200-bar median price regime, a 20-day price momentum, and a Stochastic-K midline check with a 50-bar period. Conviction score (0–3) drives a graduated allocation between the 3x leveraged ETF, the top three large-cap stocks, and cash. As with all strategies in this family, the score changes trigger rebalancing rather than price events.
452.overfit_notes: Median-of-200 regime, 20-day momentum, Stochastic(50,3,3) with K>50. The 50-bar Stochastic is a non-canonical period choice. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate

453.description: Identical in structure to the Mom20+Sto50 variant but uses a 30-day lookback for the momentum signal instead of 20. Three binary signals (median regime, 30-day momentum, Stochastic-K midline) are tallied into a conviction score that drives graduated allocation between a leveraged ETF, a large-cap stock sleeve, and cash.
453.overfit_notes: Median-of-200 regime, 30-day momentum (non-canonical), Stochastic(50,3,3) K>50. Both momentum and oscillator use non-standard periods. Rated 4/10.

454.name: Mom20+Sto5+Top3 (cc3_455.py)
454.description: A very short-term stochastic variant of the three-signal score rotator. Uses a 5-bar Stochastic (extremely fast-reacting oscillator), a 20-day momentum, and a 200-bar median regime. The speed of the Stochastic contrasts with the slower regime filter, making the combined signal sensitive to short-term overbought/oversold swings while still requiring medium-term uptrend confirmation.
454.overfit_notes: Stochastic(5,3,3) is an extremely non-canonical period. Median-of-200 regime, 20-day momentum. The 5-bar Stoch is a highly tuned choice. Rated 4/10.

481.name: PSAR+Mom20+Top3 (cc3_482.py)
481.description: A three-signal hybrid rotator that combines a Parabolic SAR trend signal with a short-term momentum check and a 200-bar median regime filter. The PSAR gives a dynamic trailing-stop-style trend reading, while the 20-day momentum adds a separate price-level confirmation. Conviction score drives graduated allocation from the leveraged ETF down through a large-cap stock sleeve to cash.
481.overfit_notes: PSAR(0.02, 0.02, 0.2) with default parameters, 20-day momentum, median-of-200 regime. Parameters are at standard defaults so overfit is modest. Rated 4/10.

482.name: Range Exp+DD60+Top3 (cc3_483.py)
482.description: A diverse three-signal rotator using market structure signals rather than momentum. The range expansion signal checks whether recent daily true ranges exceed the long-run average by at least 10%, indicating active trend conditions. The drawdown signal confirms the market is not in deep correction by requiring price within 5% of the 60-day high. The 200-bar median provides a long-run regime filter.
482.overfit_notes: Range expansion threshold of 1.1x (10% above average), 25-bar recent vs 200-bar baseline comparison; drawdown filter at 95% of 60-bar high. Three tuned thresholds. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Recent 25-bar avg true range > 200-bar avg true range × 1.1 (range expansion); price > 95% of 60-day high (drawdown check)

483.name: LR Slope+Tenkan/Kijun+Top3 (cc3_484.py)
483.description: A diverse rotator combining a linear regression slope signal with an Ichimoku Tenkan/Kijun crossover and a 200-bar median regime gate. The slope of a 50-day linear regression on closes determines if price is in an upward trend mathematically. The Tenkan (9-bar midpoint) vs Kijun (26-bar midpoint) crossover provides a separate momentum confirmation from Ichimoku theory.
483.overfit_notes: LR slope over 50 bars, Tenkan/Kijun from 9/26-bar high-low midpoints, median-of-200 regime. The LR window and Ichimoku periods are standard choices. Rated 4/10.

484.name: DD60+DD200+Top3 (cc3_485.py)
484.description: A drawdown-focused rotator that uses two separate rolling-high proximity signals (60-day and 200-day) alongside a 200-bar median regime filter. Both drawdown checks vote bullish when price remains close to recent highs at their respective horizons. This creates a layered approach where moderate corrections can still score some bullish points while severe ones turn off both signals.
484.overfit_notes: Drawdown thresholds: price/60-day high > 0.95 and price/200-day high > 0.90. Two distinct non-rounded thresholds. Rated 4/10.

485.name: Variance Ratio+Skew+Top3 (cc3_486.py)
485.description: A statistically-oriented rotator that pairs a variance ratio test (detecting trending vs mean-reverting price behavior) with a return skewness filter (positive skew indicating more frequent small losses than large gains, consistent with trending). Both signals are computed from raw return history without external indicators. A 200-bar median provides the primary regime gate.
485.overfit_notes: Variance ratio: 5-day vs 1-day variance scaled by 5, threshold > 1.0; skewness of last 60 returns > 0. Both are fully data-derived signals with no tuned thresholds beyond the zero crossing. Rated 4/10.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

486.name: OBV+Force+Top3 (cc3_487.py)
486.description: A volume-flow rotator combining two volume-based momentum signals: an On-Balance Volume slope (trending up over 30 bars) and a Force Index sum (price-change times volume over 13 bars positive). Both signals confirm that recent price moves are accompanied by supportive volume, reducing false signals from price-only momentum. The 200-bar median provides regime context.
486.overfit_notes: OBV slope window of 30 bars, Force Index sum over 13 bars — both are non-canonical period choices. Rated 4/10.

487.name: WVF+VIX-band+Top3 (cc3_488.py)
487.description: A volatility-aware hybrid rotator that uses the Williams VIX Fix (synthetic VIX computed from price data) alongside the actual CBOE VIX index. The WVF checks how far below its 22-day rolling high price sits, and the VIX level provides a macro fear filter. Together they vote that conditions are calm and risk-on before committing to leveraged exposure.
487.overfit_notes: WVF threshold < 5% (drawdown from 22-day high); VIX level threshold < 25. Both are specific numeric cutoffs that could be period-sensitive. Rated 4/10.

488.name: QQQ/TLT+Tenkan+Top3 (cc3_489.py)
488.description: A diverse rotator that cross-assets equity vs bond relative strength with an Ichimoku Tenkan/Kijun signal. The relative strength signal confirms equity is outperforming long-term Treasuries over a 60-day window before adding to risk. The Tenkan/Kijun crossover provides a shorter-term momentum confirmation. Both combine with the 200-bar median regime gate.

489.name: VolContract+Tenkan+Top3 (cc3_490.py)
489.description: A rotator pairing volatility contraction (short-term realized volatility falling below the long-run baseline) with an Ichimoku Tenkan/Kijun crossover signal. Volatility contraction tends to precede breakouts, while the Tenkan/Kijun confirms the direction. The 200-bar median provides the primary regime gate. Calm, uptrending conditions drive allocation toward the leveraged ETF.
489.overfit_notes: Vol contraction: 10-bar stddev < 60-bar stddev (rolling return series); Tenkan/Kijun from 9/26-bar midpoints; median-of-200 regime. The 10-vs-60 window pair is a tuned choice. Rated 4/10.

490.name: UpDnVol+52w+Top3 (cc3_491.py)
490.description: A volume-breadth rotator that measures whether buying pressure (up-day volume) exceeds selling pressure (down-day volume) over 20 days, combined with a 52-week proximity filter (price within 7% of the one-year high). Both signals assess market health from different angles — participation from the volume side and strength from the drawdown side — alongside the 200-bar median regime gate.
490.overfit_notes: Up-volume vs down-volume over 20 bars; drawdown threshold of 93% of 252-day high; median-of-200 regime. The 93% threshold and 252-day window are specific tuned parameters. Rated 4/10.

498.name: Mom60+Skew+Top3 (cc3_499.py)
498.description: The 60-day momentum version of the skewness-paired rotator. Uses quarterly price momentum alongside a 60-bar return skewness filter and the 200-bar median regime. The slower momentum window reduces noise while skewness tracks distributional regime shifts that often precede trend reversals. Graduated allocation shifts from leveraged ETF toward cash as signals weaken.
498.overfit_notes: 60-day momentum, 60-bar skewness > 0, median-of-200. Same skewness logic as Mom20+Skew. Rated 4/10.

The 200-bar median provides regime context.

Positive skewness confirms a distributional regime consistent with sustained uptrending. Together they identify calm, high-momentum markets.

508.name: Mom5+Mom60+Top3 (cc3_509.py)
508.description: A dual-momentum rotator that combines a very short 5-day price momentum (weekly) with a medium-term 60-day momentum, under the 200-bar median regime. The three signals vote into the standard four-tier allocation.

521.name: M20_FI_VOLCONT+Top3 (cc3_522.py)
521.description: Combines four signals — median regime, 20-day momentum, Force Index sum, and volatility contraction — into a five-tier allocation. The Force Index confirms recent price moves have volume support while volatility contraction indicates a potential breakout setup. Together with the momentum and regime gate, this targets calm, volume-confirmed momentum conditions that often precede further trend continuation.
521.overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, Force Index over 13 bars, 10-bar vs 60-bar vol contraction, median-of-200. All four in five-tier plan. Rated 4/10.

522.name: M20_FI_UPVOL+Top3 (cc3_523.py)
522.description: A four-signal volume-heavy rotator combining median regime, 20-day momentum, a Force Index sum, and an up-vs-down volume ratio. Two of the four signals are volume-based (Force Index and up/down volume), creating a strong emphasis on volume confirmation alongside price direction. Graduating allocation through five tiers allows partial exposure when volume signals are mixed.
522.overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, Force Index over 13 bars, 20-day up-volume > down-volume, median-of-200. Two distinct volume signals. Five-tier plan. Rated 4/10.

523.name: M20_NEAR60_VOLCONT+Top3 (cc3_524.py)
523.description: Combines four signals — median regime, 20-day momentum, 60-day high proximity, and volatility contraction — into a five-tier allocation. This variant emphasizes that the market should be close to recent highs (not in a drawdown) while simultaneously exhibiting volatility contraction (a potential breakout setup), with momentum confirming direction. The combination targets the quiet-but-strong trending phase.
523.overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, 95% of 60-day high, 10-bar vs 60-bar vol contraction, median-of-200. Five-tier plan with intermediate 0.7/0.3 allocations. Rated 4/10.

524.name: M20_NEAR60_UPVOL+Top3 (cc3_525.py)
524.description: A four-signal rotator combining median regime, 20-day momentum, 60-day high proximity, and a 20-day up-volume vs down-volume ratio. The proximity and volume signals both confirm market health from different dimensions: the proximity check ensures no meaningful drawdown, while the volume ratio confirms buying interest is dominant. With momentum and regime gate, these four signals together target strong sustained rallies.
524.overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, 95% of 60-day high, 20-day up-volume > down-volume, median-of-200. Five-tier plan. Rated 4/10.

525.name: M20_VOLCONT_UPVOL+Top3 (cc3_526.py)
525.description: A four-signal rotator using median regime, 20-day momentum, volatility contraction, and an up-vs-down volume ratio. Unlike the NEAR60-based variants, this strategy substitutes the proximity check with vol contraction, emphasizing the calm-before-breakout setup. Volume dominance from buyers adds a further confirmation layer. Together these four signals identify momentum conditions supported by both favorable volume flow and contracting realized volatility.
525.overfit_notes: 20-day momentum, 10-bar vs 60-bar vol contraction, 20-day up-volume > down-volume, median-of-200. Five-tier plan. Rated 4/10.
